theory_concepts.description_ja: 再利用可能なUI要素の集合。タイポ属性を含む

theory_concepts.name: Brand Typography
theory_concepts.name_ja: ブランドタイポグラフィ
theory_concepts.description: 企業・ブランドの公式的なフォント・組版規則
theory_concepts.description_ja: 企業・ブランドの公式的なフォント・組版規則

theory_concepts.name: Performance Typography
theory_concepts.name_ja: パフォーマンスタイポ
theory_concepts.description: 読み込み速度・描画負荷を考慮した最適化
theory_concepts.description_ja: 読み込み速度・描画負荷を考慮した最適化

theory_concepts.name: 字面
theory_concepts.description: The visible surface area of a character design, excluding white space within or around the glyph. Two fonts with identical point sizes may have dramatically different perceived sizes due to character face proportion.
theory_concepts.description_ja: The visible surface area of a character design, excluding white space within or around the glyph. Two fonts with identical point sizes may have dramatically different perceived sizes due to character face proportion.

theory_concepts.name: フトコロ
theory_concepts.description: The enclosed white space within and around character strokes, particularly important in Japanese typographic texture. Large, open counters increase legibility; closed counters create density.
theory_concepts.description_ja: The enclosed white space within and around character strokes, particularly important in Japanese typographic texture. Large, open counters increase legibility; closed counters create density.

theory_concepts.name: 文字面
theory_concepts.description: The proportional relationship between character width and height within the type design system. Character face proportion affects visual hierarchy, density, and reading color.
theory_concepts.description_ja: The proportional relationship between character width and height within the type design system. Character face proportion affects visual hierarchy, density, and reading color.

theory_concepts.name: モノルビ
theory_concepts.description: Ruby annotation system where single ruby character corresponds to one base character. Maintains clear one-to-one correspondence.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Ruby annotation system where single ruby character corresponds to one base character. Maintains clear one-to-one correspondence.

theory_concepts.name: グループルビ
theory_concepts.description: Ruby annotation system where multiple ruby characters correspond to a single base character. Allows more complex phonetic and semantic annotations.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Ruby annotation system where multiple ruby characters correspond to a single base character. Allows more complex phonetic and semantic annotations.

theory_concepts.name: 熟語ルビ
theory_concepts.description: Ruby annotation applied to complete idioms or multi-character word units. Essential for historical texts and specialized vocabulary.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Ruby annotation applied to complete idioms or multi-character word units. Essential for historical texts and specialized vocabulary.

theory_concepts.name: 圏点
theory_concepts.description: Typographic mark placed around or above characters to emphasize important words. Traditional in Japanese and Chinese typography.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Typographic mark placed around or above characters to emphasize important words. Traditional in Japanese and Chinese typography.

theory_concepts.name: 傍点
theory_concepts.description: Series of small dots placed directly above characters to draw attention. Commonly used in Japanese literary texts.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Series of small dots placed directly above characters to draw attention. Commonly used in Japanese literary texts.

theory_concepts.name: 縦中横
theory_concepts.description: Technique where ASCII digits or short sequences are displayed horizontally within vertically-oriented text. Critical for Japanese vertical typography.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Technique where ASCII digits or short sequences are displayed horizontally within vertically-oriented text. Critical for Japanese vertical typography.

theory_concepts.name: 連数字
theory_concepts.description: Multiple digits that function as a single unit, requiring special typographic treatment in both horizontal and vertical compositions.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Multiple digits that function as a single unit, requiring special typographic treatment in both horizontal and vertical compositions.

theory_concepts.name: 半角全角
theory_concepts.description: Distinction between narrow and full-width characters in Japanese digital typography. Modern systems integrate automatic width selection.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Distinction between narrow and full-width characters in Japanese digital typography. Modern systems integrate automatic width selection.

theory_concepts.name: 句読点処理
theory_concepts.description: Precise typographic handling of commas and periods in Japanese text, with distinct positioning rules for vertical and horizontal compositions.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Precise typographic handling of commas and periods in Japanese text, with distinct positioning rules for vertical and horizontal compositions.

theory_concepts.name: 起こし括弧
theory_concepts.description: Strategic positioning of opening brackets and parentheses at start of quotations. Follows specific typographic conventions.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Strategic positioning of opening brackets and parentheses at start of quotations. Follows specific typographic conventions.

theory_concepts.name: 受け括弧
theory_concepts.description: Precise placement of closing brackets and parentheses. Ensures visual balance with opening brackets.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Precise placement of closing brackets and parentheses. Ensures visual balance with opening brackets.

theory_concepts.name: ダーシ
theory_concepts.description: Adaptation of em-dash typography to Japanese context, creating visual breaks and transitions in horizontal text composition.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Adaptation of em-dash typography to Japanese context, creating visual breaks and transitions in horizontal text composition.

theory_concepts.name: 三点リーダー
theory_concepts.description: Typographic representation of omission or pause in Japanese text, typically using three dots or specialized character.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Typographic representation of omission or pause in Japanese text, typically using three dots or specialized character.

theory_concepts.name: 中黒
theory_concepts.description: Centered dot used to separate words or concepts in Japanese text without creating hard break. Increasingly common in digital typography.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Centered dot used to separate words or concepts in Japanese text without creating hard break. Increasingly common in digital typography.

theory_concepts.name: 波ダッシュ
theory_concepts.description: Decorative dash character used for ranges, connections, and stylistic breaks. Distinct from ASCII tilde.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Decorative dash character used for ranges, connections, and stylistic breaks. Distinct from ASCII tilde.

theory_concepts.name: 矢印類
theory_concepts.description: Strategic use of arrow glyphs and directional symbols in Japanese typography for navigation and visual flow direction.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Strategic use of arrow glyphs and directional symbols in Japanese typography for navigation and visual flow direction.

theory_concepts.name: 禁則追い込み
theory_concepts.description: Typographic technique to prevent isolated characters at line ends by compressing preceding lines. Maintains visual harmony.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Typographic technique to prevent isolated characters at line ends by compressing preceding lines. Maintains visual harmony.

theory_concepts.name: 禁則追い出し
theory_concepts.description: Deliberately loosening spacing to push orphaned characters to next line. Prevents visual awkwardness.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Deliberately loosening spacing to push orphaned characters to next line. Prevents visual awkwardness.

theory_concepts.name: ぶら下げ
theory_concepts.description: Strategic placement of punctuation outside text column to maintain visual alignment and improve reading flow.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Strategic placement of punctuation outside text column to maintain visual alignment and improve reading flow.

theory_concepts.name: 段落字下げ全角1字
theory_concepts.description: Traditional Japanese convention where first line of paragraph is indented by exactly one full-width character space.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Traditional Japanese convention where first line of paragraph is indented by exactly one full-width character space.

theory_concepts.name: 改行ルール
theory_concepts.description: Comprehensive system of rules determining where lines break and how special characters affect line continuation.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Comprehensive system of rules determining where lines break and how special characters affect line continuation.

theory_concepts.name: 箇条書き組版
theory_concepts.description: Specialized typographic treatment of bulleted or numbered lists in Japanese text composition.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Specialized typographic treatment of bulleted or numbered lists in Japanese text composition.

theory_concepts.name: 縦組み句読点位置
theory_concepts.description: Specific typographic rules for placing Japanese punctuation in vertical text layout.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Specific typographic rules for placing Japanese punctuation in vertical text layout.

theory_concepts.name: 縦組み長音
theory_concepts.description: Typographic treatment of long vowel indicator in vertical Japanese text. May display vertically or horizontally.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Typographic treatment of long vowel indicator in vertical Japanese text. May display vertically or horizontally.

theory_concepts.name: 横組み変換
theory_concepts.description: System of typographic adjustments required when converting traditionally vertical texts to horizontal composition.
theory_concepts.description_ja: System of typographic adjustments required when converting traditionally vertical texts to horizontal composition.

theory_concepts.name: 和欧混植ベースライン
theory_concepts.name_ja: 和欧混植ベースライン
theory_concepts.description: Alignment strategy for text mixing Japanese characters and Latin alphabets. Critical for modern multilingual publications.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Alignment strategy for text mixing Japanese characters and Latin alphabets. Critical for modern multilingual publications.

theory_concepts.name: 和欧混植サイズ
theory_concepts.description: Strategic sizing of Latin text within Japanese compositions to maintain visual balance. Typically 80-90% relative to Japanese height.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Strategic sizing of Latin text within Japanese compositions to maintain visual balance. Typically 80-90% relative to Japanese height.

theory_concepts.name: 字游間漢字
theory_concepts.description: Specialized kerning for kanji characters. Accounts for complex internal structure and visual weight.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Specialized kerning for kanji characters. Accounts for complex internal structure and visual weight.

theory_concepts.name: 字游間仮名
theory_concepts.description: Fine-tuning of spacing for hiragana and katakana. Maintains visual rhythm distinct from kanji.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Fine-tuning of spacing for hiragana and katakana. Maintains visual rhythm distinct from kanji.

theory_concepts.name: 行頭処理
theory_concepts.description: Typographic rules preventing certain characters from appearing at line beginning in justified composition.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Typographic rules preventing certain characters from appearing at line beginning in justified composition.

theory_concepts.name: 行末処理
theory_concepts.description: Typographic rules preventing specific characters from appearing at line end. Maintains visual balance.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Typographic rules preventing specific characters from appearing at line end. Maintains visual balance.

theory_concepts.name: 連続約物処理
theory_concepts.description: Typographic rules for managing multiple consecutive punctuation marks. Prevents visual clutter.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Typographic rules for managing multiple consecutive punctuation marks. Prevents visual clutter.

theory_concepts.name: 半角約物
theory_concepts.description: Use of narrow-width punctuation to optimize spacing in tight compositions.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Use of narrow-width punctuation to optimize spacing in tight compositions.

theory_concepts.name: フォント切替
theory_concepts.description: Strategic use of different typefaces within single text composition. Creates emphasis while maintaining coherence.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Strategic use of different typefaces within single text composition. Creates emphasis while maintaining coherence.

theory_concepts.name: 合成フォント
theory_concepts.description: Creating visual cohesion by combining characters from different typeface families. Common in multilingual typography.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Creating visual cohesion by combining characters from different typeface families. Common in multilingual typography.

theory_concepts.name: kerning
theory_concepts.description: Adjustment of space between specific character pairs to achieve optical equilibrium. Compensates for visual shapes of adjacent glyphs with uneven spacing.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Adjustment of space between specific character pairs to achieve optical equilibrium. Compensates for visual shapes of adjacent glyphs with uneven spacing.

theory_concepts.name: tracking
theory_concepts.description: Uniform adjustment of inter-character spacing across entire text block. Affects overall color and texture of typography.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Uniform adjustment of inter-character spacing across entire text block. Affects overall color and texture of typography.

theory_concepts.name: leading
theory_concepts.description: Space measured from baseline of one line to baseline of next. Crucial for readability and overall page color.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Space measured from baseline of one line to baseline of next. Crucial for readability and overall page color.

theory_concepts.name: measure
theory_concepts.description: Horizontal width of text column typically 50-75 characters per line. Too narrow creates choppy rhythm; too wide causes fatigue.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Horizontal width of text column typically 50-75 characters per line. Too narrow creates choppy rhythm; too wide causes fatigue.

theory_concepts.name: baseline
theory_concepts.description: Invisible horizontal line upon which characters sit. Fundamental to vertical rhythm and alignment across typefaces.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Invisible horizontal line upon which characters sit. Fundamental to vertical rhythm and alignment across typefaces.

theory_concepts.name: x-height
theory_concepts.description: Distance from baseline to top of lowercase letters like x. Affects perceived size and legibility at same point size.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Distance from baseline to top of lowercase letters like x. Affects perceived size and legibility at same point size.

theory_concepts.name: cap-height
theory_concepts.description: Distance from baseline to top of capital letters. Defines maximum height of standard uppercase characters.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Distance from baseline to top of capital letters. Defines maximum height of standard uppercase characters.

theory_concepts.name: ascender
theory_concepts.description: Part of lowercase letters like b, d, h that rises above x-height. Affects line spacing requirements.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Part of lowercase letters like b, d, h that rises above x-height. Affects line spacing requirements.

theory_concepts.name: descender
theory_concepts.description: Part of letters like g, j, p, q that extends below baseline. Must be considered when calculating line spacing.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Part of letters like g, j, p, q that extends below baseline. Must be considered when calculating line spacing.

theory_concepts.name: bowl
theory_concepts.description: Curved or rounded part of letters like B, P, R, a, b, d, g, o, p, q. Affects character recognition and visual weight.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Curved or rounded part of letters like B, P, R, a, b, d, g, o, p, q. Affects character recognition and visual weight.

theory_concepts.name: counter
theory_concepts.description: Enclosed or partially enclosed white space within letterform like interior of A, B, D, O. Critical for legibility.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Enclosed or partially enclosed white space within letterform like interior of A, B, D, O. Critical for legibility.

theory_concepts.name: terminal
theory_concepts.description: End of stroke not finished with serif particularly relevant in sans-serif and slab-serif designs.
theory_concepts.description_ja: End of stroke not finished with serif particularly relevant in sans-serif and slab-serif designs.

theory_concepts.name: serif
theory_concepts.description: Small line or curve projecting from end of letterform stroke. Associated with traditional formal typography.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Small line or curve projecting from end of letterform stroke. Associated with traditional formal typography.

theory_concepts.name: sans
theory_concepts.description: Typeface category without serifs at character terminals. Associated with modern clean minimalist design.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Typeface category without serifs at character terminals. Associated with modern clean minimalist design.

theory_concepts.name: stroke-contrast
theory_concepts.description: Ratio between thickest and thinnest strokes in typeface. High contrast creates elegance; low creates simplicity.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Ratio between thickest and thinnest strokes in typeface. High contrast creates elegance; low creates simplicity.

theory_concepts.name: stress
theory_concepts.description: Apparent angle of emphasis in curved strokes particularly visible in rounded characters. Affects typeface personality.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Apparent angle of emphasis in curved strokes particularly visible in rounded characters. Affects typeface personality.

theory_concepts.name: axis
theory_concepts.description: Imaginary line through thinnest parts of curved strokes. Determines stroke stress and character personality.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Imaginary line through thinnest parts of curved strokes. Determines stroke stress and character personality.

theory_concepts.name: bracket
theory_concepts.description: Transitional curve connecting serif to main stroke of letter. Affects visual weight and elegance.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Transitional curve connecting serif to main stroke of letter. Affects visual weight and elegance.

theory_concepts.name: foot
theory_concepts.description: Base or bottom portion of letterform including any serif. Essential for baseline alignment and stability.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Base or bottom portion of letterform including any serif. Essential for baseline alignment and stability.

theory_concepts.name: beak
theory_concepts.description: Sharp or pointed terminal found on certain letters. Adds character personality but affects legibility.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Sharp or pointed terminal found on certain letters. Adds character personality but affects legibility.

theory_concepts.name: spur
theory_concepts.description: Sharp outward projection from interior curve of letter. Contributes to character distinctiveness.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Sharp outward projection from interior curve of letter. Contributes to character distinctiveness.

theory_concepts.name: ligature-ff
theory_concepts.description: Combined f-f letterform preventing visual collision. Many professional fonts include ligatures.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Combined f-f letterform preventing visual collision. Many professional fonts include ligatures.

theory_concepts.name: ligature-fi
theory_concepts.description: Combined f-i letterform preventing dot crowding. Essential for professional typography.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Combined f-i letterform preventing dot crowding. Essential for professional typography.

theory_concepts.name: ligature-fl
theory_concepts.description: Combined f-l letterform creating visual harmony. Common in professional typography systems.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Combined f-l letterform creating visual harmony. Common in professional typography systems.

theory_concepts.name: small-caps
theory_concepts.description: Uppercase letters scaled to x-height size. Creates sophisticated typographic texture.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Uppercase letters scaled to x-height size. Creates sophisticated typographic texture.

theory_concepts.name: oldstyle-figures
theory_concepts.description: Numerals with varying heights resembling lowercase letters. Integrate seamlessly with body text.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Numerals with varying heights resembling lowercase letters. Integrate seamlessly with body text.

theory_concepts.name: proportional-figures
theory_concepts.description: Numerals with varying widths optimized for word text. Creates natural reading flow.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Numerals with varying widths optimized for word text. Creates natural reading flow.

theory_concepts.name: tabular-figures
theory_concepts.description: Numerals with identical widths designed for tables. Essential for financial documents.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Numerals with identical widths designed for tables. Essential for financial documents.

theory_concepts.name: swash-characters
theory_concepts.description: Stylistic variants featuring extended decorative flourishes. Used for initials and luxury design.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Stylistic variants featuring extended decorative flourishes. Used for initials and luxury design.

theory_concepts.name: optical-kerning
theory_concepts.description: Automatic kerning based on character shapes rather than predefined pairs.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Automatic kerning based on character shapes rather than predefined pairs.

theory_concepts.name: metric-kerning
theory_concepts.description: Kerning based on predefined kern pair metrics embedded in font file.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Kerning based on predefined kern pair metrics embedded in font file.

theory_concepts.name: hanging-punctuation
theory_concepts.description: Punctuation placed outside text column boundary. Maintains visual alignment.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Punctuation placed outside text column boundary. Maintains visual alignment.

theory_concepts.name: optical-alignment
theory_concepts.description: Fine-tuning character positioning based on visual perception not mathematical alignment.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Fine-tuning character positioning based on visual perception not mathematical alignment.

theory_concepts.name: optical-sizes
theory_concepts.description: Font designs optimized for specific typographic sizes with adjusted contrast and spacing.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Font designs optimized for specific typographic sizes with adjusted contrast and spacing.

theory_concepts.name: ink-trap
theory_concepts.description: Recessed area in character design compensating for ink spread during printing.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Recessed area in character design compensating for ink spread during printing.

theory_concepts.name: super-script
theory_concepts.description: Characters displayed in reduced size and positioned above baseline for footnotes.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Characters displayed in reduced size and positioned above baseline for footnotes.

theory_concepts.name: sub-script
theory_concepts.description: Characters displayed in reduced size and positioned below baseline for formulas.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Characters displayed in reduced size and positioned below baseline for formulas.

theory_concepts.name: fraction-glyph
theory_concepts.description: Single pre-drawn glyph representing complete fraction. Improves readability.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Single pre-drawn glyph representing complete fraction. Improves readability.

theory_concepts.name: ordinal
theory_concepts.description: Typographic representation of ordinal numbers using raised letters or superscript.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Typographic representation of ordinal numbers using raised letters or superscript.

theory_concepts.name: em-quad
theory_concepts.description: Space unit equal to one em width. Fundamental for modular spacing systems.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Space unit equal to one em width. Fundamental for modular spacing systems.

theory_concepts.name: em-dash-usage
theory_concepts.description: Long horizontal line for breaks in thought and ranges of numbers.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Long horizontal line for breaks in thought and ranges of numbers.

theory_concepts.name: en-dash-usage
theory_concepts.description: Medium dash for ranges and to indicate relationships between items.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Medium dash for ranges and to indicate relationships between items.

theory_concepts.name: smart-quotes
theory_concepts.description: Quotation marks that adjust direction based on opening or closing context.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Quotation marks that adjust direction based on opening or closing context.

theory_concepts.name: prime-glyph
theory_concepts.description: Symbol for feet inches and minutes in mathematical and scientific notation.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Symbol for feet inches and minutes in mathematical and scientific notation.

theory_concepts.name: apostrophe
theory_concepts.description: Punctuation mark for contractions and possessives. Distinct from quotes and primes.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Punctuation mark for contractions and possessives. Distinct from quotes and primes.

theory_concepts.name: page-proportion
theory_concepts.description: Aspect ratio of page fundamental to overall design harmony. Common proportions include golden ratio.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Aspect ratio of page fundamental to overall design harmony. Common proportions include golden ratio.

theory_concepts.name: golden-ratio
theory_concepts.description: Proportion of 1 to 1.618 found in nature and classical architecture. Creates visual harmony.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Proportion of 1 to 1.618 found in nature and classical architecture. Creates visual harmony.

theory_concepts.name: root-rectangle
theory_concepts.description: Page proportion system based on root values. Creates modular mathematically consistent design.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Page proportion system based on root values. Creates modular mathematically consistent design.

theory_concepts.name: modular-scale
theory_concepts.description: Series of proportionally related sizes creating coherent visual hierarchy.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Series of proportionally related sizes creating coherent visual hierarchy.

theory_concepts.name: hierarchy-levels
theory_concepts.description: Structured system defining multiple levels of visual prominence.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Structured system defining multiple levels of visual prominence.

theory_concepts.name: vertical-rhythm
theory_concepts.description: System where line heights and spacing follow consistent mathematical rhythm.
theory_concepts.description_ja: System where line heights and spacing follow consistent mathematical rhythm.

theory_concepts.name: baseline-grid-alignment
theory_concepts.description: System where all text baselines align to invisible grid.
theory_concepts.description_ja: System where all text baselines align to invisible grid.

theory_concepts.name: white-space
theory_concepts.description: Deliberate use of empty space to create breathing room.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Deliberate use of empty space to create breathing room.

theory_concepts.name: negative-space
theory_concepts.description: Space around and between elements that define their shape.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Space around and between elements that define their shape.

theory_concepts.name: asymmetric-balance
theory_concepts.description: Visual equilibrium through dissimilar elements with equal weight.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Visual equilibrium through dissimilar elements with equal weight.

theory_concepts.name: symmetric-balance
theory_concepts.description: Design balance through identical or mirror-image positioning.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Design balance through identical or mirror-image positioning.

theory_concepts.name: focal-point
theory_concepts.description: Primary visual target drawing viewer's eye first.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Primary visual target drawing viewer's eye first.

theory_concepts.name: visual-flow
theory_concepts.description: Sequence and direction of visual elements.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Sequence and direction of visual elements.

theory_concepts.name: reading-path
theory_concepts.description: Deliberate arrangement of text to guide reader through content.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Deliberate arrangement of text to guide reader through content.

theory_concepts.name: gestalt-grouping
theory_concepts.description: Application of Gestalt principles to organize elements.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Application of Gestalt principles to organize elements.

theory_concepts.name: figure-ground
theory_concepts.description: Perceptual separation of primary from secondary elements.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Perceptual separation of primary from secondary elements.

theory_concepts.name: contrast-strategies
theory_concepts.description: Systematic approaches to creating visual distinction.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Systematic approaches to creating visual distinction.

theory_concepts.name: color-hierarchy
theory_concepts.description: Use of color properties to establish visual hierarchy.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Use of color properties to establish visual hierarchy.

theory_concepts.name: size-hierarchy
theory_concepts.description: Establishing importance through systematic size relationships.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Establishing importance through systematic size relationships.

theory_concepts.name: weight-hierarchy
theory_concepts.description: Use of font weight and stroke thickness for hierarchy.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Use of font weight and stroke thickness for hierarchy.

theory_concepts.name: position-hierarchy
theory_concepts.description: Establishing importance through positioning conventions.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Establishing importance through positioning conventions.

theory_concepts.name: texture-contrast
theory_concepts.description: Creating contrast through visual texture differences.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Creating contrast through visual texture differences.

theory_concepts.name: density-contrast
theory_concepts.description: Visual contrast through element concentration variation.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Visual contrast through element concentration variation.

theory_concepts.name: cluster-formation
theory_concepts.description: Strategic spatial grouping of related elements.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Strategic spatial grouping of related elements.

theory_concepts.name: break-formation
theory_concepts.description: Deliberate spacing between distinct content areas.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Deliberate spacing between distinct content areas.

theory_concepts.name: visual-rhythm
theory_concepts.description: Cohesion through systematic repetition of elements.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Cohesion through systematic repetition of elements.

theory_concepts.name: musical-meter
theory_concepts.description: Applying musical timing principles to visual rhythm.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Applying musical timing principles to visual rhythm.

theory_concepts.name: phrase-formation
theory_concepts.description: Organizing content into meaningful chunks.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Organizing content into meaningful chunks.

theory_concepts.name: emphasis-techniques
theory_concepts.description: Systematic approaches to emphasizing important content.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Systematic approaches to emphasizing important content.

theory_concepts.name: coherence-strategies
theory_concepts.description: Methods ensuring design elements work together harmoniously.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Methods ensuring design elements work together harmoniously.

theory_concepts.name: unity-strategies
theory_concepts.description: Approaches ensuring diverse elements feel like unified whole.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Approaches ensuring diverse elements feel like unified whole.

theory_concepts.name: proportion-systems
theory_concepts.description: Mathematical frameworks for proportional relationships.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Mathematical frameworks for proportional relationships.

theory_concepts.name: saccade
theory_concepts.description: Quick involuntary eye movements when eye moves from one fixation point to another. Occur 3-8 times per second.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Quick involuntary eye movements when eye moves from one fixation point to another. Occur 3-8 times per second.

theory_concepts.name: fixation
theory_concepts.description: Period when eye remains stationary focused on specific area. Lasts 100-300 milliseconds during reading.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Period when eye remains stationary focused on specific area. Lasts 100-300 milliseconds during reading.

theory_concepts.name: legibility
theory_concepts.description: Degree to which individual characters can be clearly distinguished. Concerned with character recognition.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Degree to which individual characters can be clearly distinguished. Concerned with character recognition.

theory_concepts.name: readability
theory_concepts.description: Degree to which continuous text can be comfortably understood. Encompasses legibility plus spacing.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Degree to which continuous text can be comfortably understood. Encompasses legibility plus spacing.

theory_concepts.name: comprehension
theory_concepts.description: Level at which readers understand and retain meaning. Ultimate goal of typography.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Level at which readers understand and retain meaning. Ultimate goal of typography.

theory_concepts.name: contrast-threshold
theory_concepts.description: Minimum visual difference perceivable by eye. Essential for accessibility.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Minimum visual difference perceivable by eye. Essential for accessibility.

theory_concepts.name: WCAG-AA
theory_concepts.description: Industry standard accessibility criteria ensuring contrast ratios of at least 4.5:1.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Industry standard accessibility criteria ensuring contrast ratios of at least 4.5:1.

theory_concepts.name: dyslexia-friendly
theory_concepts.description: Design strategies addressing dyslexic reading challenges. Benefits all readers.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Design strategies addressing dyslexic reading challenges. Benefits all readers.

theory_concepts.name: low-vision
theory_concepts.description: Design approaches for readers with reduced visual acuity.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Design approaches for readers with reduced visual acuity.

theory_concepts.name: fovea
theory_concepts.description: Central area of retina responsible for detailed vision. Covers only 1-2 degrees.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Central area of retina responsible for detailed vision. Covers only 1-2 degrees.

theory_concepts.name: peripheral-vision
theory_concepts.description: Outer area of visual perception. Important for motion detection.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Outer area of visual perception. Important for motion detection.

theory_concepts.name: eye-strain
theory_concepts.description: Physical discomfort from prolonged reading or visual focus.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Physical discomfort from prolonged reading or visual focus.

theory_concepts.name: leading-effect
theory_concepts.description: Significant influence of leading on reading comfort and comprehension.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Significant influence of leading on reading comfort and comprehension.

theory_concepts.name: line-length-effect
theory_concepts.description: Influence of text column width on reading speed.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Influence of text column width on reading speed.

theory_concepts.name: measure-perception
theory_concepts.description: How readers perceive and evaluate column width.
theory_concepts.description_ja: How readers perceive and evaluate column width.

theory_concepts.name: recognition-vs-discrimination
theory_concepts.description: Distinction between recognizing versus distinguishing from something else.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Distinction between recognizing versus distinguishing from something else.

theory_concepts.name: word-shape
theory_concepts.description: Outline silhouette of word formed by irregular letter tops.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Outline silhouette of word formed by irregular letter tops.

theory_concepts.name: syllable-recognition
theory_concepts.description: Cognitive processing of syllable units within words.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Cognitive processing of syllable units within words.

theory_concepts.name: subvocalization
theory_concepts.description: Tendency for readers to internally vocalize text while reading.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Tendency for readers to internally vocalize text while reading.

theory_concepts.name: silent-reading
theory_concepts.description: Reading practice where no sound is produced.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Reading practice where no sound is produced.

theory_concepts.name: depth-of-processing
theory_concepts.description: Degree to which readers engage cognitively with text.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Degree to which readers engage cognitively with text.

theory_concepts.name: glance-comprehension
theory_concepts.description: Ability to rapidly extract meaning through quick visual scanning.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Ability to rapidly extract meaning through quick visual scanning.

theory_concepts.name: sustained-reading
theory_concepts.description: Extended reading where full attention directs at comprehension.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Extended reading where full attention directs at comprehension.

theory_concepts.name: scanning-mode
theory_concepts.description: Reading strategy for locating specific information.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Reading strategy for locating specific information.

theory_concepts.name: searching-mode
theory_concepts.description: Reading mode investigating visual content for relevance.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Reading mode investigating visual content for relevance.

theory_concepts.name: interruption-recovery
theory_concepts.description: Cognitive capacity to resume after interruption.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Cognitive capacity to resume after interruption.

theory_concepts.name: reading-fatigue
theory_concepts.description: Cognitive fatigue from extended reading sessions.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Cognitive fatigue from extended reading sessions.

theory_concepts.name: Tinker-1963
theory_concepts.description: Landmark research establishing readable typography principles.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Landmark research establishing readable typography principles.

theory_concepts.name: Bouma-1971
theory_concepts.description: Research on word recognition based on outline shapes.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Research on word recognition based on outline shapes.

theory_concepts.name: Smith-1971
theory_concepts.description: Research on reading psychology and comprehension.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Research on reading psychology and comprehension.

theory_concepts.name: screen-rendering
theory_concepts.description: Process of converting digital typography to visible pixels. Involves rasterization and anti-aliasing.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Process of converting digital typography to visible pixels. Involves rasterization and anti-aliasing.

theory_concepts.name: hinting
theory_concepts.description: Adding instructions to fonts to align characters optimally to pixel grids.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Adding instructions to fonts to align characters optimally to pixel grids.

theory_concepts.name: anti-aliasing
theory_concepts.description: Rendering technique softening jagged edges in rasterization.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Rendering technique softening jagged edges in rasterization.

theory_concepts.name: sub-pixel-rendering
theory_concepts.description: Advanced rendering using RGB channels for three times effective resolution.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Advanced rendering using RGB channels for three times effective resolution.

theory_concepts.name: ClearType
theory_concepts.description: Microsoft's sub-pixel rendering implementation in Windows.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Microsoft's sub-pixel rendering implementation in Windows.

theory_concepts.name: VF-wght
theory_concepts.description: Variable font weight axis controlling stroke thickness continuously.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Variable font weight axis controlling stroke thickness continuously.

theory_concepts.name: VF-wdth
theory_concepts.description: Variable font width axis controlling character width.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Variable font width axis controlling character width.

theory_concepts.name: VF-opsz
theory_concepts.description: Variable font optical size axis automatically adjusting design.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Variable font optical size axis automatically adjusting design.

theory_concepts.name: VF-ital
theory_concepts.description: Variable font italic axis controlling italicization degree.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Variable font italic axis controlling italicization degree.

theory_concepts.name: VF-slnt
theory_concepts.description: Variable font slant axis controlling slant angle.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Variable font slant axis controlling slant angle.

theory_concepts.name: OpenType-SVG
theory_concepts.description: Font format using SVG graphics for color character design.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Font format using SVG graphics for color character design.

theory_concepts.name: WOFF2
theory_concepts.description: Modern web font format with 30-50% compression.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Modern web font format with 30-50% compression.

theory_concepts.name: font-display-swap
theory_concepts.description: CSS property displaying fallback text during font loading.
theory_concepts.description_ja: CSS property displaying fallback text during font loading.

theory_concepts.name: font-display-block
theory_concepts.description: CSS value keeping text invisible during font loading.
theory_concepts.description_ja: CSS value keeping text invisible during font loading.

theory_concepts.name: FOUT
theory_concepts.description: Web typography where text displays in fallback fonts briefly.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Web typography where text displays in fallback fonts briefly.

theory_concepts.name: FOIT
theory_concepts.description: Web typography where text remains invisible until fonts load.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Web typography where text remains invisible until fonts load.

theory_concepts.name: system-font-stack
theory_concepts.description: CSS relying on pre-installed system fonts.
theory_concepts.description_ja: CSS relying on pre-installed system fonts.

theory_concepts.name: prefers-reduced-motion
theory_concepts.description: CSS media query detecting animation preferences.
theory_concepts.description_ja: CSS media query detecting animation preferences.

theory_concepts.name: WCAG-2-2
theory_concepts.description: Latest version of web accessibility standards.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Latest version of web accessibility standards.

theory_concepts.name: ARIA-roles
theory_concepts.description: Accessibility markup roles for assistive technologies.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Accessibility markup roles for assistive technologies.

theory_concepts.name: text-resize
theory_concepts.description: Accessibility requirement for enlarged text readability.
theory_concepts.description_ja: Accessibility requirement for enlarged text readability.

theory_concepts.name: zoom-text-only
theory_concepts.description: CSS functionality for independent text zoom.
theory_concepts.description_ja: CSS functionality for independent text zoom.

theory_concepts.name: line-height-CSS

grid_systems.name: Japanese Vertical Writing Grid
grid_systems.description: 漢字・仮名を支える縦組みグリッド。「のど」（背側）と「小口」（表側）の非対称マージン。天地の空間美学。
grid_systems.description_ja: 漢字・仮名を支える縦組みグリッド。「のど」（背側）と「小口」（表側）の非対称マージン。天地の空間美学。

grid_systems.name: Japanese Horizontal Writing Grid
grid_systems.description: 戦後普及の横組みグリッド。欧文構造に準じつつ、日本語の行長最適値（35～40字）を保つ。
grid_systems.description_ja: 戦後普及の横組みグリッド。欧文構造に準じつつ、日本語の行長最適値（35～40字）を保つ。

grid_systems.name: Japanese Vertical + Horizontal Mixed Grid
grid_systems.description: 縦組み・横組み混在。タイトル・本文・キャプションで異なる方向。複雑だが情報階層を明確化。
grid_systems.description_ja: 縦組み・横組み混在。タイトル・本文・キャプションで異なる方向。複雑だが情報階層を明確化。

grid_systems.name: Japanese 2-Column Vertical Grid
grid_systems.description: 縦組み2段。古い新聞・雑誌の標準。右から左へのページめくり。1段15～20字推奨。
grid_systems.description_ja: 縦組み2段。古い新聞・雑誌の標準。右から左へのページめくり。1段15～20字推奨。

grid_systems.name: Sugiura Modular System
grid_systems.name_ja: 杉浦康平 モジュール・システム
grid_systems.description: 日本の代表的グラフィックデザイナーの理論。1文字を基本モジュール。漢字・仮名の共存構造。余白の積極的活用。
grid_systems.description_ja: 日本の代表的グラフィックデザイナーの理論。1文字を基本モジュール。漢字・仮名の共存構造。余白の積極的活用。

grid_systems.name: Modular Grid
grid_systems.name_ja: モジュラー・グリッド
grid_systems.description: 垂直列と水平行の両方を持つグリッド。方形のモジュール。最高の柔軟性。複数の情報タイプに対応。
grid_systems.description_ja: 垂直列と水平行の両方を持つグリッド。方形のモジュール。最高の柔軟性。複数の情報タイプに対応。

grid_systems.name: Hierarchical Grid
grid_systems.description: 異なるサイズのモジュールを組み合わせたグリッド。主要要素・副要素の視覚的階層化。複雑な情報構造に最適。
grid_systems.description_ja: 異なるサイズのモジュールを組み合わせたグリッド。主要要素・副要素の視覚的階層化。複雑な情報構造に最適。

grid_systems.name: Baseline Grid
grid_systems.name_ja: ベースライン・グリッド
grid_systems.description: リーディング（行間）を基本単位とする水平ガイド。すべてのテキストがこのグリッドにアライン。DTP環境の標準。
grid_systems.description_ja: リーディング（行間）を基本単位とする水平ガイド。すべてのテキストがこのグリッドにアライン。DTP環境の標準。

grid_systems.name: Compound Grid
grid_systems.description: 複数のグリッドを重ね合わせたシステム。各グリッドが異なる機能を果たす。最高の柔軟性と表現力。
grid_systems.description_ja: 複数のグリッドを重ね合わせたシステム。各グリッドが異なる機能を果たす。最高の柔軟性と表現力。

grid_systems.name: Manuscript Grid
grid_systems.name_ja: マニュスクリプト・グリッド
grid_systems.description: 中世写本の版面設計。手書き文字の配置基準。幾何学的な美しさ。活字印刷の原型。
grid_systems.description_ja: 中世写本の版面設計。手書き文字の配置基準。幾何学的な美しさ。活字印刷の原型。

grid_systems.name: Golden Ratio Grid (1.618)
grid_systems.description: 黄金比φ=1.618による版面構造。もっとも高級感のある比率。古典理論との親和性が高い。
grid_systems.description_ja: 黄金比φ=1.618による版面構造。もっとも高級感のある比率。古典理論との親和性が高い。

grid_systems.name: Square Root 2 Grid (1.414)
grid_systems.description: ISO用紙比率 A4/B5 の√2=1.414。国際標準との統一。印刷効率の最適化。
grid_systems.description_ja: ISO用紙比率 A4/B5 の√2=1.414。国際標準との統一。印刷効率の最適化。

grid_systems.name: Perfect Fifth Grid (1.5)
grid_systems.description: 音楽の完全5度（3:2=1.5）による比率。調和的でありながら計算しやすい。実装性に優れる。
grid_systems.description_ja: 音楽の完全5度（3:2=1.5）による比率。調和的でありながら計算しやすい。実装性に優れる。

grid_systems.name: Major Third Grid (1.25)
grid_systems.description: 音楽の長3度（5:4=1.25）による比率。温和で読みやすい。デジタルインターフェース向け。
grid_systems.description_ja: 音楽の長3度（5:4=1.25）による比率。温和で読みやすい。デジタルインターフェース向け。

grid_systems.name: Modular Scale Grid (Composite)
grid_systems.name_ja: モジュラースケール・グリッド
grid_systems.description: 複数の比率（黄金比・5度・3度など）を組み合わせたスケール。複数要素の調和を実現。
grid_systems.description_ja: 複数の比率（黄金比・5度・3度など）を組み合わせたスケール。複数要素の調和を実現。

grid_systems.name: CSS Grid Layout
grid_systems.description: W3C CSS Grid Layout Module。2-次元のグリッド配置。コンテナクエリ対応。最新のウェブ標準。
grid_systems.description_ja: W3C CSS Grid Layout Module。2-次元のグリッド配置。コンテナクエリ対応。最新のウェブ標準。

grid_systems.name: Flexbox Grid
grid_systems.description: CSS Flexible Box Layout。1次元のフレキシブル配置。均等分割・比例配置に優れる。
grid_systems.description_ja: CSS Flexible Box Layout。1次元のフレキシブル配置。均等分割・比例配置に優れる。

grid_systems.name: Bootstrap Grid (12-column)
grid_systems.description: ウェブフレームワークBootstrapの標準12段グリッド。レスポンシブブレークポイント。実装定番。
grid_systems.description_ja: ウェブフレームワークBootstrapの標準12段グリッド。レスポンシブブレークポイント。実装定番。

grid_systems.name: Tailwind Grid
grid_systems.description: ユーティリティファーストCSSフレームワーク。12段グリッド。細粒度の制御。プロパティベース。
grid_systems.description_ja: ユーティリティファーストCSSフレームワーク。12段グリッド。細粒度の制御。プロパティベース。

grid_systems.name: Material Design Grid
grid_systems.description: Google Material Designの統一グリッド。4dp単位。モバイル・デスクトップ統一。システム思考。
grid_systems.description_ja: Google Material Designの統一グリッド。4dp単位。モバイル・デスクトップ統一。システム思考。

grid_systems.name: Fibonacci Grid
grid_systems.name_ja: フィボナッチ・グリッド
grid_systems.description: フィボナッチ数列（1,1,2,3,5,8,13...）による比率。自然界に最も多く現れるパターン。有機的な感覚。
grid_systems.description_ja: フィボナッチ数列（1,1,2,3,5,8,13...）による比率。自然界に最も多く現れるパターン。有機的な感覚。

grid_systems.name: Canon of Three (3:4:5)
grid_systems.description: 古典幾何学の3:4:5直角三角形による比例。ピタゴラスの定理。建築由来。
grid_systems.description_ja: 古典幾何学の3:4:5直角三角形による比例。ピタゴラスの定理。建築由来。

grid_systems.name: Octave Grid (2.0)
grid_systems.name_ja: オクターブ・グリッド（2:1）
grid_systems.description: 音楽のオクターブ（2:1=2.0）。最もシンプルな比率。倍と半分の関係。デジタルスケーリングに最適。
grid_systems.description_ja: 音楽のオクターブ（2:1=2.0）。最もシンプルな比率。倍と半分の関係。デジタルスケーリングに最適。

grid_systems.name: Rule of Thirds Grid
grid_systems.description: 写真・絵画の構図法。ページを3×3に分割。交点に主要要素を配置。シンプルで効果的。
grid_systems.description_ja: 写真・絵画の構図法。ページを3×3に分割。交点に主要要素を配置。シンプルで効果的。

grid_systems.name: Log Scale Grid
grid_systems.name_ja: 対数スケール・グリッド
grid_systems.description: 対数スケールによるグリッド。データ可視化・インフォグラフィクス向け。指数的増減の表現。
grid_systems.description_ja: 対数スケールによるグリッド。データ可視化・インフォグラフィクス向け。指数的増減の表現。

schools_movements.name: New Typography
schools_movements.name_ja: ニュー・タイポグラフィ
schools_movements.description: Modernist typography movement emphasizing sans-serif, asymmetric layout, and functional design
schools_movements.description_ja: モダニズム・タイポグラフィ運動。サンセリフ、非対称レイアウト、機能性を重視
schools_movements.key_figures: Jan Tschichold, El Lissitzky, Herbert Bayer, László Moholy-Nagy
schools_movements.key_principles: asymmetric composition, sans-serif preference, mixed case, grid-based

schools_movements.name: Swiss Style
schools_movements.description: International typographic style with rigorous grids, sans-serif, and rational design
schools_movements.description_ja: 国際タイポグラフィック・スタイル。厳密なグリッド、サンセリフ、合理的設計
schools_movements.key_figures: Josef Müller-Brockmann, Karl Gerstner, Armin Hofmann, Emil Ruder
schools_movements.key_principles: grid systems, Helvetica/Akzidenz, mathematical proportion, neutrality

schools_movements.name: Postmodern Typography
schools_movements.name_ja: ポストモダン・タイポグラフィ
schools_movements.description: Reaction against Swiss rigor; embraces deconstruction, layering, and emotion
schools_movements.description_ja: スイス派への反動。脱構築・レイヤー・感情の復権
schools_movements.key_figures: David Carson, Neville Brody, Wolfgang Weingart, Edward Fella
schools_movements.key_principles: deconstruction, layered composition, expressive type, rule-breaking

schools_movements.name: Japanese Book Design (Sotei)
schools_movements.description: Japanese tradition of book design integrating calligraphy, illustration, and binding craft
schools_movements.description_ja: 書道・装画・製本工芸を統合する日本の装幀伝統
schools_movements.key_figures: Onchi Koshiro, Hiraga Kohga, Sugiura Kohei, Sobue Shin
schools_movements.key_principles: calligraphic harmony, paper-binding craft, restrained ornament

schools_movements.name: Penguin Composition Rules
schools_movements.name_ja: ペンギン・コンポジション・ルール
schools_movements.description: Tschichold's typographic standard for Penguin Books, widely emulated
schools_movements.description_ja: Tschicholdが定めたPenguin Booksの組版規範
schools_movements.key_principles: justified setting, classical proportion, Pelican logo system

schools_movements.name: Werkbund
schools_movements.description: German alliance of artists, architects, designers integrating craft and industry
schools_movements.description_ja: ドイツ工作連盟。芸術家・建築家・デザイナーの工芸と産業の統合運動
schools_movements.key_figures: Hermann Muthesius, Henry van de Velde, Peter Behrens

schools_movements.name: Constructivist Books

layout_canons.name: Van de Graaf canon
layout_canons.description: Renaissance geometric page construction discovered by Jan Tschichold and rediscovered by Hendrik van de Graaf, dividing page diagonals into 9 segments to derive type area with 1:1.5:2 margin ratios. Universal across page sizes.
layout_canons.construction_method: Draw both page diagonals; divide page width by 9; intersect with diagonals to define text block. Type area sits asymmetrically with smaller inner margin and largest bottom margin, optimizing for codex spread reading.

layout_canons.name: Tschichold golden canon
layout_canons.description: Tschichold's modernization of medieval canons in The Form of the Book (1975), preserving 2:3 page ratio with 9-division margins, derived from manuscripts of the high Middle Ages.
layout_canons.construction_method: Page proportion 2:3. Inner margin = 1 unit, top = 1, outer = 2, bottom = 3. Type block height equals page width. Text fits diagonals; ensures harmony with page proportions.

layout_canons.name: Rosarivo canon
layout_canons.description: Raul Rosarivo's 1947 empirical analysis of incunabula, establishing the geometric law that text height equals page width and proportional margin scheme 2:3:4:6.
layout_canons.construction_method: Divide page width by 9 to get base unit. Inner=2, top=3, outer=4, bottom=6 units. Text block proportions match the page itself, scaling fractally. Used in Gutenberg Bible analysis.

layout_canons.name: Villard de Honnecourt scheme
layout_canons.description: 13th century Gothic page construction by architect Villard de Honnecourt using diagonal subdivision to harmonize text with page proportions, predating Renaissance canons.
layout_canons.construction_method: Pages divided into 9 cells via diagonal lines forming X pattern. Text block occupies 6 of 9 cells in central position, leaving asymmetric margins favoring outer and lower areas for thumb space.

layout_canons.name: Le Corbusier Modulor
layout_canons.description: Le Corbusier's 1948 anthropomorphic measurement system applying golden ratio to human proportions for architecture and book design, creating modular scales of harmonious dimensions.
layout_canons.construction_method: Two scales (Red and Blue) based on average male body height (1.83m) and golden ratio. Dimensions cascade through phi multiplications. Applied to book grids, page heights, type sizes for total proportional harmony.

layout_canons.name: Gutenberg canon
layout_canons.description: 42-line Bible (1455) page construction following medieval canons of harmonic proportion, with text occupying ~50% of page area and asymmetric margins favoring outer.
layout_canons.construction_method: Two-column text block on 2:3 page. Inner margins minimized for binding, outer enlarged for thumb. Type area hugs upper-inner area, bottom margin largest. Established Western book design conventions.

layout_canons.name: Vitruvius proportions
layout_canons.description: Vitruvian classical architectural proportions from De Architectura applied to book pages, establishing geometric ratios derived from human body and golden mean.
layout_canons.construction_method: Apply golden ratio (phi=1.618) to page width:height. Text block follows phi or its inverse. Margins use Fibonacci sequence relationships. Roots all classical typography in classical order.

layout_canons.name: Greek classical golden ratio
layout_canons.description: Ancient Greek phi-based proportional system foundational to Western design, producing visually harmonious rectangles and divisions used in scrolls and later codex pages.
layout_canons.construction_method: Page or text block in 1:phi ratio. Cuts and subdivisions follow golden section. Margins reflect golden-ratio decreasing sequence. Foundation of Renaissance and modern proportional canons.

layout_canons.name: Roman classical canon
layout_canons.description: Imperial Roman scriptio continua page layout with capitalis quadrata script, no word spaces, generous margins, and stately uniform letter heights.
layout_canons.construction_method: Wide outer margins, narrow inner. Single column scriptio continua. Capital letters in uniform height. Wax tablets and papyrus scrolls dictated horizontal-only reading. Established imperial readability standards.

layout_canons.name: Carolingian minuscule canon
layout_canons.description: Charlemagne-era 8th-9th century reform unifying European script into clear minuscule with consistent x-height, word spacing, and balanced margins.
layout_canons.construction_method: Clear word separation introduced. Minuscule script with consistent baseline. Margins asymmetric with larger outer for marginalia. Established readable book hand basis for all Western typography.

layout_canons.name: Gothic textura canon
layout_canons.description: 13th-15th century Northern European Gothic black-letter page with dense vertical strokes, narrow columns, and minimal margins to economize parchment.
layout_canons.construction_method: Two narrow columns, tight leading. Textura quadrata with diamond serifs. Margins reduced versus Carolingian. High contrast between thick verticals and hairlines. Vellum economy favors density.

layout_canons.name: Renaissance Italianate
layout_canons.description: 15th-16th century Italian humanist book canon by Aldus Manutius and contemporaries, reviving Roman capital and minuscule with elegant margins and italic for portability.
layout_canons.construction_method: Single column. Roman or italic faces. Lower margin notably wider. Page size shrinks to enchiridion (pocket) format. Classical proportions in 1:phi or 2:3. Italic accommodates more text per line.

layout_canons.name: Jenson canon
layout_canons.description: Nicolas Jenson's 1470 Venetian Roman type page exemplifying classical proportions, used by Bruce Rogers as model for Centaur typeface and modern fine-press design.
layout_canons.construction_method: Single block, generous margins. Type area approximately 0.6× page. Bottom margin 1.5× top. Used phi divisions. Type x-height moderate, ascenders prominent. Foundation of fine-press tradition.

layout_canons.name: Aldus Manutius pocket book canon
layout_canons.description: Aldus's 1501 Octavo format introducing portable books with italic type and tight margins, democratizing reading and shaping book scales for centuries.
layout_canons.construction_method: Octavo format ~108×170mm. Italic typeface for line economy. Margins reduced for portability while preserving readability. Top binding allowed reading without table. Established travel-book conventions.

layout_canons.name: Bodoni 1818 canon
layout_canons.description: Giambattista Bodoni's Manuale Tipografico (1818) establishing Modern (Didone) type with extreme contrast, vertical stress, and stark white space typography.
layout_canons.construction_method: Wide margins emphasizing white space. Bodoni typeface with high stroke contrast. Single column, generous leading. Page proportions tend square. White space treated as integral design element.

layout_canons.name: Didot 1810 canon
layout_canons.description: Firmin Didot's French rationalist Modern type with vertical stress and high contrast, applied to neoclassical book design with measured geometric proportions.
layout_canons.construction_method: Symmetric layout with rationalized margins. Didot type with thin hairlines. Standardized page dimensions (Didot point 0.376mm). Classical balanced composition; foundation of French publishing.

layout_canons.name: Penguin 1935 standard canon
layout_canons.description: Edward Young's Penguin Books 1935 design with horizontal tri-band cover (color/title/black), Gill Sans typography, establishing mass-market paperback aesthetic.
layout_canons.construction_method: Horizontal three bands on cover (top color, white middle, bottom color). Gill Sans titling. Single-column body in Bembo or Pilgrim. Tight A-format paperback dimensions 110×178mm. Democratic, affordable design.

layout_canons.name: Penguin Composition Rules 1947
layout_canons.description: Jan Tschichold's 4-year tenure (1947-49) at Penguin establishing tight rules for type composition: spacing, ligatures, hyphenation, justification, capitalization.
layout_canons.construction_method: Tight letterspacing only on caps. Ligatures preserved. Em-dash with no space. Type size 11pt minimum. Generous leading. Single column with classical 2:3 page. Rules codified in 4-page memo, became typographic gospel.

layout_canons.name: Pelican Books canon
layout_canons.description: Penguin sister imprint (1937-1984) for serious nonfiction with distinctive light-blue covers, scholarly typography by Hans Schmoller (post-Tschichold), academic margins.
layout_canons.construction_method: Light-blue covers with white horizontal band. Times Roman or Pilgrim body type. Wider outer margins for note-taking. 11pt body, 13pt leading. Educational tone via spacious composition.

layout_canons.name: Faber & Faber editorial canon
layout_canons.description: Berthold Wolpe's 40-year (1941-75) Faber design tradition using Albertus titling with painterly covers, establishing British literary publisher aesthetic.
layout_canons.construction_method: Albertus titling for covers. Hand-lettered or typographic only covers (no images often). Body in Granjon, Bembo, Caslon. Generous margins for poetry collections. Square-ish format for verse.

layout_canons.name: 和漢古典版面（巻子本）
layout_canons.description: Heian-Muromachi era Japanese scroll page composition with vertical writing in distinct columns, kana flowing freely between fixed kanji rhythms, ink-shade variations.
layout_canons.construction_method: 縦書き複数列、行送りは流動的。漢字は固定リズム、仮名は連綿で流れる。墨の濃淡で強弱。料紙の継ぎ目を意識した構成。上下余白は装飾余白として機能し、左右は連続する。源氏物語絵巻等の伝統。

layout_canons.name: 写経古典版面（経典）
layout_canons.description: Buddhist sutra copying tradition with strict columnar composition, gold/silver mica grounds, vermilion punctuation, and precise vertical alignment for liturgical use.
layout_canons.construction_method: 縦17字×17列が標準（千字経等）。罫線は金泥または銀泥。写経独特の楷書体。朱の界線で行を区切る。上下余白は段ごとの呼吸を作る。料紙は紺紙金字、白紙黒字等。荘厳的な厳密性。

layout_canons.name: 江戸版本版面（黄表紙）
layout_canons.description: Edo period popular book genre (1775-1806) with woodblock printed integrated text-image compositions, kana-rich text flowing around illustrations.
layout_canons.construction_method: 半紙判、和綴じ。文字と絵が版面で一体化。仮名主体、絵を囲むように文が配置。物語の流れに応じて余白が変動。版木彫りの線質が活字より柔らかい。挿絵は文字と等価な情報。

layout_canons.name: 明治活版版面（東京活版）
layout_canons.description: Meiji-era introduction of Western movable type with first Japanese fonts (Tsukiji Type Foundry) establishing modern vertical typography conventions.
layout_canons.construction_method: 明朝体活字（東京築地活版製造所）。縦組み二段組が基本。約物処理を欧文に倣って規格化。上下マージンを欧米式に。漢字は明朝、仮名は変体仮名から平仮名へ統一。新聞・雑誌の規格を作った。

layout_canons.name: 写研写植版面（昭和）
layout_canons.description: Showa-era photo-typesetting (1960s-90s) by Shaken (Sha-Ken) enabling fine kerning, rubies, and vertical sophistication in magazines and books.
layout_canons.construction_method: 写研フォント（石井明朝、ナール、ゴナ等）。級数（Q）単位の精細な指定。手作業に近い字游間調整。雑誌の凝った組版が可能に。和文ルビの精緻な処理。1990s以降のDTP移行で衰退するが伝統技法は今も参照される。

layout_canons.name: 杉浦康平モジュラーグリッド
layout_canons.description: Sugiura Kohei's modular grid system synthesizing Swiss systematic design with East Asian cosmological ordering, applied to encyclopedic books and magazines.
layout_canons.construction_method: アジア的世界観をスイス・グリッドに統合。曼荼羅的多層構造。情報を等価レイヤーで配置。文字と図像を等価に扱うグリッド。『全宇宙誌』『遊』で実装。多重に重なるグリッドが情報の宇宙誌を構築する。

layout_canons.name: 鈴木一誌ページネーション基本
layout_canons.description: Suzuki Hitoshi's Pagination for the Basic Manual (1990s) systematizing 180 page design rules for Japanese editorial publishing, foundational reference.
layout_canons.construction_method: 180項目のページ設計ルール体系。版面率、字詰め、行送り、約物処理を体系化。メディアごとの最適値を提示。実務的かつ理論的。日本のエディトリアルデザインの理論的支柱。雑誌や書籍の現場マニュアルとして広く使われる。

layout_canons.name: Müller-Brockmann grid systems
layout_canons.description: Josef Müller-Brockmann's 1981 Grid Systems in Graphic Design codifying Swiss modular grids with mathematical precision, foundational for international typographic style.
layout_canons.construction_method: 列数・行数を数学的に決定。フィールド単位での情報配置。マージンとガターの厳密な比例。Akzidenz Grotesk/Helveticaを基本書体に。中立的・機能主義的。スイス派の聖典として現代まで参照される。

layout_canons.name: Marber Penguin Crime grid (1961)
layout_canons.description: Romek Marber's 1961 grid for Penguin Crime Series creating modular cover system with horizontal bands separating type, image, and price for instant brand recognition.
layout_canons.construction_method: カバー上3分の1にタイトル+著者ゴシック、中央に画像、下にロゴ・価格・シリーズ。Marber gridとして類書に展開。シリーズ性とブランド統一。Romek Marberが1961年に開発、シリーズ300+冊で実装。

layout_canons.name: Hofmann Basel canon
layout_canons.description: Armin Hofmann's Basel School pedagogical method emphasizing reduction, geometric simplicity, and rhythmic spacing in posters and book design (1947-1985).
layout_canons.construction_method: 幾何学的削減。ポイント・ライン・サーフェスの基本原理。リズミカルな空間配分。バーゼルスクールの教育課題と実践を融合。Akzidenz Grotesk中心。Wolfgang Weingartらに伝播し、ポスト・モダン理論へ展開。
